allowed_claim: cuFlye can opt-in dump and validate a deterministic read-to-graph overlap input-boundary oracle before chainReadAlignments, with stable raw overlap and chain_input records, while preserving exact toy-hifi Flye canonical artifacts.
forbidden_claim: M6a does not add a CUDA kernel, prove default GPU mode, prove whole-Flye speedup, or prove that GPU-generated read-to-graph overlaps can be consumed by graph logic.
next_recommended_step: M6b: build an external replay/packing harness for this input-boundary ABI so a CUDA candidate/minimizer prototype can reproduce the chain_input records before Flye consumes GPU output.
plain_language_benefit: M6a moved the CUDA target earlier than read-alignment chain DP. The dump shows toy-hifi spends about 1.55-1.59 seconds in quick read-to-graph overlap discovery versus about 0.17 seconds in divergence filtering and under 1 ms in chain DP, so the next useful GPU work should target candidate/minimizer discovery, not the already tiny chain-DP slice.
